title: गणेश कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश कवचम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री गणपतिकवचस्य नारद ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। श्री महागणपतिर्देवता।
गं बीजम्। स्वाहा शक्तिः। गणपतये इति कीलकम्।
श्री गणपतिप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
रक्तवर्णं चतुर्बाहुं पाशाङ्कुशधरं प्रभुम्।
मोदकं दन्तमेकं च धारयन्तं कराम्बुजे॥
मूषकस्थं महाकायं मोदकासक्तमानसम्।
ध्यायेत्सर्वगणाधीशं विघ्नविध्वंसकारकम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥

गणेशो मस्तकं पातु ललाटं गणनायकः।
नेत्रे मे पातु विघ्नेशो गणराजः श्रुती सदा॥१॥

घ्राणं पातु गणश्रेष्ठो मुखं तु सुमुखः सदा।
जिह्वां पातु गणक्रीडो दन्तान् पातु गजाननः॥२॥

कण्ठं पातु गणाधीशो स्कन्धौ विघ्नहरो मम।
भुजौ पातु गजस्कन्धो हस्तौ मे पातु सिद्धिदः॥३॥

वक्षः पातु गणाध्यक्षो हृदयं गणपूजितः।
उदरं लम्बतुण्डश्च कटिं पातु गजप्रियः॥४॥

ऊरू मे पातु विकटो जानुनी पातु मंगलः।
जंघे पातु गजक्रीडो गुल्फौ विघ्नप्रणाशनः॥५॥

पादौ मे पातु सर्वज्ञो विघ्नश्चापगतो मम।
सर्वाङ्गं मे सदा पातु विनायकमहाबलः॥६॥

पूर्वस्यां पातु हेरम्बो ह्याग्नेयां लम्बकर्णकः।
दक्षिणस्यां गजग्रीवो नैरृत्यां तु गणाधिपः॥७॥

पश्चिमे पातु विघ्नेशो वायव्यां पातु गणप्रभुः।
उदीच्यां पातु विकटो ऐशान्यां लम्बजठरः॥८॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु गजाननो ह्यधस्ताद्विष्णुवल्लभः।
दिवा पातु महाबुद्धी रात्रौ पातु गणेश्वरः॥९॥

सन्ध्ययोः पातु विश्वेशो राजद्वारे गणाधिपः।
संग्रामे पातु विघ्नेशो विजयं दिशतु प्रभुः॥१०॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
एतत् कवचमज्ञात्वा यो जपेद्गणपतिम्।
न तस्य जायते सिद्धिरभिचारे च पौष्टिके॥

ज्ञात्वा कवचमेतत्तु पठेद्यो भक्तिसंयुतः।
तस्य विघ्नाः प्रनश्यन्ति सर्वकार्याणि साधयेत्॥

सर्वविघ्नप्रशमनं सर्वशत्रुविनाशनम्।
सर्वसिद्धिप्रदं शुद्धं कवचं गणपस्य तु॥

॥ इति श्री गणेशपुराणोक्तं श्री गणेश कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganesha Kavacham ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Ganapati-kavachasya Narada Rishiph.
Gayatri Chhandah. Shri Maha-Ganapatihr Devata.
Gam Bijam. Svaha Shaktih. Ganapataye Iti Kilakam.
Shri Ganapati-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Rakta-varnam Chatur-bahum Pasha-ankusha-dharam Prabhum.
Modakam Dantam-ekam cha Dharayantam Karambuje.
Mushaka-stham Mahakayam Modaka-asakta-manasam.
Dhyayet-sarva-gana-adhisham Vighna-vidhvamsa-karakam.

|| Kavacham ||

Ganesho Mastakam Patu Lalatam Gana-nayakah.
Netre Me Patu Vighnesho Ganarajah Shruti Sada.||1||

Ghranam Patu Gana-shreshtho Mukham Tu Sumukhah Sada.
Jihvam Patu Gana-krido Dantan Patu Gajananah.||2||

Kantham Patu Gana-adhisho Skandhau Vighna-haro Mama.
Bhujau Patu Gaja-skandho Hastau Me Patu Siddhi-dah.||3||

Vakshah Patu Gana-adhyaksho Hridayam Gana-pujitah.
Udaram Lamba-tundash-cha Katim Patu Gaja-priyah.||4||

Uru Me Patu Vikato Januni Patu Mangalah.
Janghe Patu Gaja-krido Gulphau Vighna-pranashanah.||5||

Padau Me Patu Sarvajno Vighnash-chapa-gato Mama.
Sarvangam Me Sada Patu Vinayaka-mahabalah.||6||

Purvasyam Patu Herambo Hy-agneyam Lamba-karnakah.
Dakshinasyam Gaja-grivo Nairityam Tu Gana-adhipah.||7||

Pashchime Patu Vighnesho Vayavyam Patu Gana-prabhuh.
Udichyam Patu Vikato Aishanyam Lamba-jatharah.||8||

Urdhvam Patu Gajanano Hy-adhastad-Vishnu-vallabhah.
Diva Patu Mahabuddhi Ratrau Patu Ganeshvarah.||9||

Sandhyayoh Patu Vishvesho Rajadvare Gana-adhipah.
Sangrame Patu Vighnesho Vijayam Dishatu Prabhuh.||10||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Etat Kavacham-ajnatva Yo Japed-Ganapatim.
Na Tasya Jayate Siddhir-abhichare cha Paushtike.

Jnatva Kavacham-etat-tu Pathed-yo Bhakti-samyutah.
Tasya Vighnah Pranashyanti Sarva-karyani Sadhayet.

Sarva-vighna-prashamanam Sarva-shatru-vinashanam.
Sarva-siddhi-pradam Shuddham Kavacham Ganapasya Tu.

|| Iti Shri Ganesha-puranoktam Shri Ganesha Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान — रक्त (लाल) वर्ण के, चार भुजाओं वाले, पाश-अंकुश धारण करने वाले, मोदक और एक दन्त हाथ में धारण करने वाले, मूषक पर स्थित, महाकाय, मोदक में आसक्त मन वाले, सर्वगणाधीश, विघ्नविध्वंसक गणपति का ध्यान करें।

मस्तक की गणेश, ललाट की गणनायक, नेत्रों की विघ्नेश, कानों की गणराज रक्षा करें। नासिका की गणश्रेष्ठ, मुख की सुमुख, जिह्वा की गणक्रीड, दन्तों की गजानन रक्षा करें।

कण्ठ की गणाधीश, कन्धों की विघ्नहर, भुजाओं की गजस्कन्ध, हस्तों की सिद्धिद, वक्ष की गणाध्यक्ष, हृदय की गणपूजित, उदर की लम्बतुण्ड, कटि की गजप्रिय रक्षा करें।

पूर्व में हेरम्ब, दक्षिण में गजग्रीव, पश्चिम में विघ्नेश, उत्तर में विकट सदा रक्षा करें। ऊपर गजानन, नीचे विष्णुवल्लभ, दिन में महाबुद्धि, रात में गणेश्वर रक्षा करें।

इस कवच को जानकर जो भक्तिपूर्वक पाठ करता है, उसके सब विघ्न नष्ट होते हैं और सभी कार्य सिद्ध होते हैं।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीगणपतिकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्रीमहागणपतिर्देवता। गं बीजम्। स्वाहा शक्तिः। गणपतये कीलकम्। श्रीमहागणपतिप्रीत्यर्थे गणेशकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides complete divine armor protection of Lord Ganesha
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles from career, education, and personal life
Traditionally invoked for protection from all directions and at all times (day, night, sandhya)
Essential prerequisite before Ganesha mantra japa for full efficacy
Traditionally recited for removal of all enemies and negative influences
Invokes all siddhis and ensures success in all undertakings
description: Ganesha Kavacham — गणेश कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Ganesha. This sacred composition is sourced from Ganesha Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all enemies and negative influences and protect from all directions and at all times (day, night, sandhya). It is also said to remove all obstacles from career, education, and personal life. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesha Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ganesha Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred armor hymn of Lord Ganesha for divine protection and obstacle removal.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Ganesh Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The most widely circulated Ganesh Kavacham is preserved in the Ganesha Purana, where it is taught by Sage Narada to seekers desiring complete protection through Lord Ganapati. The viniyoga clearly identifies Narada as the rishi, Gayatri as the chhand, Maha-Ganapati as the devata, the bija as gam, the shakti as svaha and the kilaka as ganapataye. A second, longer Ganesh Kavacham is found in the Mudgala Purana, and shorter armor verses appear within the Ganapati Atharvashirsha tradition. Devotees in Maharashtra and Karnataka most commonly recite the Ganesha Purana version of ten to twelve verses as part of daily Ganapati upasana. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Greenmesg; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ganesh Kavacham structured?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacham follows the classical anga-nyasa pattern in which each named form of Ganesha is invoked to protect a specific limb. Ganesha protects the head, Gananayaka the forehead, Vighnesha the eyes, Ganaraja the ears, Ganashreshtha the nose, Sumukha the mouth, Gajanana the teeth, Ganadhisha the throat, Vighnahara the shoulders, Siddhida the hands, Lambatunda the belly, Vikata the thighs, Mangala the knees and Sarvajna the feet. Subsequent verses cover the eight directions with Heramba in the east, Lambakarnaka in the southeast and so on, plus protection in day, night, sandhya, royal court and battlefield. This complete envelopment is what makes a kavacham distinct from a simple stotra. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Greenmesg; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Ganesh Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Budhvar) is the weekday traditionally assigned to Lord Ganapati, and most devotees include the kavacham in their morning Wednesday puja. Chaturthi tithi of every lunar fortnight, especially Sankashta Chaturthi in the krishna paksha and Vinayaka Chaturthi in the shukla paksha, is highly auspicious. The annual peak occasions are Ganesh Chaturthi in Bhadrapada and the ten-day Ganeshotsav. Many practitioners also chant the kavacham as a protective shield before commencing Ganapati mula-mantra japa, before travel, before examinations and before signing any new contract or undertaking. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ganesh Chaturthi; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Ganesh Kavacham and Sankashta Nashana Ganesha Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sankashta Nashana Ganesha Stotram, found in the Narada Purana, is a praise hymn that lists twelve names of Ganesha and is recited specifically to remove sankata or acute distress; its phalashruti promises that even chanting once destroys obstacles. The Ganesh Kavacham, by contrast, is an armor invocation in which each verse appoints a specific form of Ganesha to guard a specific limb or direction. Devotees often combine the two, reciting the Sankashta Nashana Stotram for active obstacle removal in a current crisis and the kavacham daily as a preventive protective shield. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Greenmesg.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ganesh Kavacham phalashruti promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Ganesha Purana version states that one who recites this kavacham with bhakti has all obstacles destroyed, accomplishes every undertaking, achieves victory over enemies and is granted every siddhi. It explicitly notes that those who attempt abhichara (hostile rituals) or paushtika (nourishing rites) without first knowing this kavacham fail to attain results, while those who recite it gain success in both. Beyond worldly benefits, daily recitation is said to purify the devotee's intelligence, develop fearlessness in royal courts and battlefields, and ultimately lead to Ganapati-loka after death. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Vedadhara Ganesha Purana; Greenmesg.)

seoFaqs.question: Can the Ganesh Kavacham be recited by anyone, including women and children?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. Unlike some Vedic mantra recitations that traditionally require initiation, the Ganesh Kavacham is a Pauranika stotra and is open to all devotees regardless of gender, age or background. Children are commonly taught the kavacham before examinations and competitions, women recite it during Sankashta Chaturthi vrat for family welfare, and householders include it in morning sandhya. The only customary requirement is cleanliness of body and place, sitting facing east or north, and offering durva grass, red flowers or modaka along with the recitation. (Sources: Hindupedia; Drikpanchang; Greenmesg.)

seoFaqs.question: How long does it take to chant the Ganesh Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard Ganesha Purana version comprises approximately ten verses plus the dhyana sloka, viniyoga and phalashruti, and takes about seven to nine minutes to recite at a steady devotional pace. The longer Mudgala Purana version, which expands the directional protection and adds more named forms of Ganesha, runs to fifteen to twenty minutes. For daily practice most devotees use the shorter Ganesha Purana text, while for special occasions like Ganesh Chaturthi or Sankashta Chaturthi vrat the longer Mudgala version may be preferred. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Greenmesg.)
